title: [Bug]: OCI Permission Denied when mapping uids and mounting nested NFS volumes
body:
### Issue Description

When using mapping uids to have a non root user inside the container be mapped to the rootless outside user together with mounting two volumes from an rfs into a nested folder structure inside the container, there is an OCI permission denied error.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Set up a nfs4 mount under `/nfs` on the host machine
2. Create a Docker image `custom_user_image ` that starts as the user with uid 1234
3. Try running `podman run --rm --uidmap 0:1:1234 --uidmap 1234:0:1 --uidmap 1235:1235:64302 -v /nfs/folder_A/:/toplevel -v /nfs/folder_B:/toplevel/subdir custom_user_image ls -la /toplevel/subdir`

_uids were mapped according to https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#39-podman-run-fails-with-error-unrecognized-namespace-mode-keep-iduid1000gid1000-passed_


### Describe the results you received

```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 248397b6b856 skipped: already exists
Copying blob bb2453e12947 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 636a428ce0f7 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 7354e83da007 skipped: already exists
Copying blob fa1175420e6f skipped: already exists
Copying blob c284f546974c skipped: already exists
Copying blob 8b0860f70229 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 1741b53163b6 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 580e8400d508 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 4efcd4003c84 skipped: already exists
Copying config 996583328c done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: error during container init: error mounting "/nfs/folder_B" to rootfs at "/toplevel/subdir": lstat /podman/root/storage/overlay/fda19c135ee072f554c0b5393083729f27e7ad87e969ec9044ba3ce5ee2c4a2b/merged/toplevel/subdir: permission denied: OCI permission denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

No error

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Works fine when mounting the directories as siblings of each other. 
Works fine when the volume used is not nfs.

comments.body:
that is sort of expected since NFS is not user namespace aware and DAC_OVERRIDE is not honored.

You've three users that are stepping on each other: 1) root in the rootless user namespace (your UID), 2) root in the container and 3) user 1024 in the container.  Depending how the OCI runtime decides to create the `subdir` directory, you'll see this kind of weird errors since it is assumed DAC_OVERRIDE works on the underlying file system.

You may try to precreate `subdir` on volumeA and make it world writeable, but besides this attempt, there is not much more you can do.
@giuseppe , it would be very useful to mount NFS volumes and use the user id mapping. Is there any chance that this will be supported in the future? Would it be worth to file a feature request?
That depends exclusively on the kernel, there is nothing we can do from userspace
@JWollnik I would doubt that this will ever be supported.
